content.full_text:
January 29, 1990

SECRETARY OF STATE
Political Reform Division
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

Re: Request of opinion.

Dear Sir or Madam:

I am requesting an opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) in respect to the following facts:

I am the treasure and sole committee member of a Political Action Committee (PAC) which currently has funds to utilize in this respect. It is a non-controlled committee. The committee is identified as, "COMMITTEE FOR THE INDEPENDENCE OF DOWNEY COURT", founded January 21, 1988, FPPC number: 880210.

Currently there are potential (declared) candidates to fill a vacancy and an anticipated retirement vacancy in the Downey Municipal Court.

One of the individuals who has stated his intention to run for the vacancy is a bench officers (Commissioner) sitting in the Downey Municipal Court.

He has asked me to serve as his treasurer. While I have no personal problem in serving as his treasurer, I am disturbed by the potential, if not actual (certainly the possible appearance), of a conflict of interest. Clearly his committee would be a controlled committee.

Question:

As treasure, of the PAC can I give any of these funds to this candidate?

If so, is there a limit on the amount?

Assuming the answer is, "yes" (that I can give either all or a portion of the PAC funds to this candidate) would this affirmative answer be changed in any respect if I were to also serve as treasure of the recipient election committee?

The requested opinion also needs to consider the situation of whether I could (without a conflict) serve as the candidates treasure, if the PAC funds (all or part if United) were to be transferred to the election committee before I were to become the treasurer of the election committee; or, would this circumstance make any difference?

I do not mean to be critical but I have received several and somewhat equivocal response to these questions, via telephone calls to FPPC. Because of this I am requesting a written opinion to assure that all actions taken (if in fact any of the described actions can be taken) are without taint and are completely proper. Thank You.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
LEO B. NEWTON
Attorney at Law

cc: REGISTRAR-RECORDER
    County of Los Angeles
